params.text:
Reading and completing a MAR chart

The Medication Administration Record (MAR) chart is the legal record of the medicines a person has been prescribed and what has actually been given. Good recording protects the person and protects you.

  • Record on the MAR chart as soon as the medicine has been taken — never in advance.
  • Write legibly, sign each entry, and use the correct date and time.
  • Avoid abbreviations and keep entries factual.
  • Use the correct code if a dose is not given — for example refused, not available, or hospital — rather than leaving a blank.

A gap on the MAR chart is a serious problem: if there is no record, it must be assumed the medicine was not given.

params.alt: Illustration for MAR charts and recording

params.taskDescription: Drag each word to the correct gap to complete these MAR chart recording rules.
params.textField: Complete the *MAR* chart as soon as the medicine has been *taken*, never in advance. Each entry must be *signed* and written *legibly* so it can be read clearly. If a dose is not given, record the correct *code* instead of leaving a *blank*.
